 {"id":"wa-2l27x.7","title":"E2E test suite & scripts with detailed logging","description":"End-to-end test scripts that verify the full session persistence lifecycle, with detailed structured logging for diagnosis.

## E2E test script: scripts/e2e_session_persistence.sh

### Test harness setup
- Creates isolated FT_WORKSPACE in /tmp/ft-e2e-session-XXXXXX
- Starts a real WezTerm mux server (or uses existing)
- Starts ft watch in foreground mode with --log-level=debug --log-format=json
- All output captured to $WORKSPACE/logs/

### Test scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Basic checkpoint-crash-restore cycle
```
1. Start ft watch
2. Create 3 panes via wezterm cli split-pane
3. cd to different directories in each pane
4. Wait for at least 2 periodic checkpoints
5. Verify: ft session list shows 1 active session with 3 panes
6. Kill ft watch with SIGKILL (simulates crash — no graceful shutdown)
7. Verify: database has shutdown_clean = 0
8. Start ft watch again
9. Verify: restore prompt appears (or auto-restores if configured)
10. Verify: 3 new panes created with correct cwds
11. Verify: pane ID mapping recorded in checkpoint metadata
```
Exit: PASS/FAIL with detailed log output on failure

#### Scenario 2: Complex topology preservation
```
1. Create a 2x3 grid (2 rows, 3 columns via nested splits)
2. Set unique titles and cwds for each pane
3. Checkpoint
4. Kill + restart
5. Verify: all 6 panes restored with correct positions
6. Verify: split proportions within 10% of original
```

#### Scenario 3: Partial restore resilience
```
1. Create 4 panes
2. Checkpoint
3. Kill ft watch
4. Delete one of the cwds (rm -rf one test directory)
5. Restart ft watch with restore
6. Verify: 3 panes restored, 1 skipped with warning
7. Verify: restore completes (doesn't abort on partial failure)
```

#### Scenario 4: Checkpoint deduplication efficiency
```
1. Create 2 panes, wait for steady state
2. Record checkpoint count over 10 seconds
3. Verify: only 1-2 checkpoints written (dedup working)
4. Send text to a pane (changes state)
5. Verify: new checkpoint written immediately (event trigger)
```

#### Scenario 5: Graceful shutdown vs crash
```
1. Start ft watch, create panes
2. ft stop (graceful)
3. Verify: shutdown_clean = 1 in database
4. Restart
5. Verify: NO restore prompt (clean shutdown)
```

#### Scenario 6: Multiple crash recovery
```
1. Create session, checkpoint, SIGKILL
2. Restart, restore
3. Add more panes, checkpoint, SIGKILL again
4. Restart, restore
5. Verify: latest topology (with added panes) is restored
6. Verify: ft session list shows correct history
```

#### Scenario 7: Checkpoint retention
```
1. Set checkpoint_retention = 3
2. Trigger 10 checkpoints (with state changes)
3. Verify: only 3 checkpoints remain in database
4. Verify: oldest checkpoints and their pane_state rows are deleted
```

### Logging verification
Each scenario verifies that expected log lines appear in the JSON log output:
- Checkpoint written log with pane count and byte size
- Checkpoint skipped log (dedup)
- Restore detected log with session ID
- Pane restore log with old_id → new_id mapping
- Error/warning logs for partial failures

### Script output format
```
═══════════════════════════════════════
ft Session Persistence E2E Test Suite
═══════════════════════════════════════

[1/7] Basic checkpoint-crash-restore ............ PASS (3.2s)
[2/7] Complex topology preservation ............ PASS (4.1s)
[3/7] Partial restore resilience ............... PASS (2.8s)
[4/7] Checkpoint deduplication ................. PASS (12.1s)
[5/7] Graceful shutdown vs crash ............... PASS (2.5s)
[6/7] Multiple crash recovery .................. PASS (5.3s)
[7/7] Checkpoint retention .................... PASS (8.2s)

═══════════════════════════════════════
Results: 7/7 passed, 0 failed
Logs: /tmp/ft-e2e-session-abc123/logs/
═══════════════════════════════════════
```

### Failure output
On failure, automatically dumps:
- Last 50 lines of ft watch JSON log
- ft session list output
- ft session doctor output
- Database schema version and table row counts

## Integration with CI
- Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml as a separate job
- Requires WezTerm installed (feature-gated, skip if not available)
- Timeout: 5 minutes

 {"id":"wa-2l27x.8","title":"FrankenSQLite migration plan","description":"Plan and prepare for eventual migration from rusqlite to frankensqlite as the storage backend.

## Precondition
frankensqlite (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite) must complete Phase 5+ (WAL/B-tree/MVCC wired as default execution backend, not just tested components).

## Migration benefits
- MVCC concurrent writers: 50+ agent panes writing simultaneously without single-writer bottleneck
- RaptorQ self-healing WAL: survives torn writes and bit-flips after power loss
- Native mode time-travel: replay any terminal session at any point in time
- XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption at rest (session state is sensitive)

## Migration tasks (when ready)
1. Add frankensqlite as workspace dependency (it's a 23-crate workspace — evaluate binary size impact)
2. Create storage backend trait to abstract rusqlite vs frankensqlite
3. Implement frankensqlite backend behind feature flag
4. Benchmark: single-writer throughput, concurrent writer throughput, checkpoint latency
5. Migration tool: convert existing rusqlite .db to frankensqlite format
6. Gradual rollout: feature flag default off, test in staging

 {"id":"wa-2l27x.9","title":"Graceful shutdown checkpoint & signal handlers","description":"Implement signal handlers that trigger a final checkpoint with type='shutdown' before ft exits, so restarts after clean shutdown don't trigger restore prompts.

## Signal handling
- Register handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT (Ctrl-C), and SIGHUP
- On signal received:
  1. Log INFO: "Shutdown signal received ({signal}), writing final checkpoint"
  2. Trigger one final checkpoint via the checkpoint engine's event channel
  3. Wait for checkpoint to complete (with 5-second timeout)
  4. Set mux_sessions.shutdown_clean = 1
  5. Log INFO: "Clean shutdown complete, session {id} saved"
  6. Proceed with normal shutdown (stop watcher, close connections)

## Implementation
- Use tokio::signal for async signal handling
- The checkpoint engine (.4) already handles event-triggered checkpoints — send a ShutdownCheckpoint event
- Add a shutdown_tx oneshot channel that the signal handler awaits for confirmation
- If checkpoint times out (5s), log WARN and proceed with shutdown anyway (don't hang)

## ft stop command integration
- ft stop sends SIGTERM to the watcher process
- The watcher's signal handler triggers the shutdown checkpoint
- ft stop waits up to 10 seconds for clean exit, then SIGKILL

## Edge cases
- Double SIGINT (user hits Ctrl-C twice): second signal forces immediate exit without checkpoint
- Signal during an in-progress checkpoint: wait for current checkpoint, skip the shutdown checkpoint (already have recent state)
- Signal before first checkpoint: write a checkpoint even if checkpoint_enabled=false in config

## Unit tests required
- test_shutdown_sets_clean_flag: SIGTERM → shutdown_clean = 1
- test_shutdown_checkpoint_written: verify checkpoint with type='shutdown' in database
- test_shutdown_timeout: slow checkpoint → timeout → still exits cleanly
- test_double_sigint: second signal forces immediate exit
- test_ft_stop_triggers_clean_shutdown: ft stop → watcher exits with shutdown_clean = 1

## Logging
- INFO: "Shutdown signal received ({signal}), writing final checkpoint"
- INFO: "Clean shutdown complete, session {session_id} saved ({pane_count} panes)"
- WARN: "Shutdown checkpoint timed out after 5s, exiting without final save"
- WARN: "Double signal received, forcing immediate exit"
- DEBUG: "Shutdown checkpoint written: checkpoint_id={id}, elapsed={ms}ms"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-10T23:45:51.036219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T22:26:34.408533-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T22:26:34.408533-05:00","close_reason":"shutdown_checkpoint() method in snapshot_engine.rs + main.rs watcher lifecycle wiring complete (30f22fc, 4f83353)","labels":["runtime","shutdown","signals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.9","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:45:51.036219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-2l27x.9","depends_on_id":"wa-2l27x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:46:41.220989Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-2l9kn","title":"Integrate cass (session search) for agent context injection","description":"# Integrate cass for Agent Context Injection

## Skills: /idea-wizard, /extreme-software-optimization

## What cass does
Searches and indexes coding agent session history across 10+ AI tools. Sub-60ms search with Tantivy FTS, BM25 ranking, optional semantic embeddings.

## Current state
Standalone CLI at /Users/jemanuel/projects/coding_agent_session_search.

## Integration plan

### 1. Import cass-core as library
- Use cass search engine directly, not via subprocess
- Share the SQLite/Tantivy index (read-only from FrankenTerm)

### 2. Automatic context injection
When a new agent pane starts work on a task:
- Query cass for related past sessions (by bead ID, file paths, error messages)
- Inject top-3 relevant session excerpts as initial context
- Reduces "cold start" problem where agents re-discover solutions

### 3. On-error search
When FrankenTerm detects an error pattern in pane output:
- Automatically search cass for similar past errors
- If a solution was found before, inject it as a hint
- MCP tool: agent can explicitly search cass

### 4. Swarm learning
- After an agent solves a problem, index the session into cass
- Future agents automatically benefit from collective knowledge
- FrankenTerm orchestrates the index-then-search cycle

### 5. Performance (/extreme-software-optimization)
- Search latency: <100ms for top-10 results
- Index update: background, non-blocking
- Memory: shared index, not per-pane copies

## Tests
- Test context injection: new pane gets relevant history
- Test on-error search: error pattern triggers cass query
- Test search latency under 200-pane load (criterion benchmarks)
- Test index freshness: new session indexed within 60s
- **Test swarm learning cycle**: Agent A solves error → indexed → Agent B hits same error → gets Agent A solution as hint. Verify end-to-end with mock sessions.
- **Test index contention**: 200 panes querying and 10 panes indexing simultaneously, verify no deadlock or degradation

## Acceptance criteria
- cass-core as library dependency
- Automatic context injection on pane start
- On-error pattern search
- MCP tool for explicit search
- Swarm learning: solve→index→search cycle verified end-to-end
- <100ms search latency under concurrent load

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Search latency benchmarks:
  - Query latency: benchmark search for varying index sizes (1K, 10K, 100K sessions). Target <60ms p50, <100ms p99.
  - Concurrent search: benchmark 200 concurrent queries against a 100K-session index. Verify no degradation beyond 2x single-query latency.
  - Index write throughput: benchmark indexing new sessions. Target >100 sessions/sec.
  - Context injection: benchmark the full injection pipeline (search + rank + format top-3). Target <200ms end-to-end.
- **Proptest for search result relevance**: Property-based tests for search quality:
  - Generate random session documents with known keywords, search for those keywords, and verify the matching documents appear in the top-10 results
  - Generate pairs of semantically similar sessions (same error message, different context), verify both are returned when searching for the error
  - Generate sessions with no matching content, verify search returns empty results (no false positives from index corruption)
  - For BM25 ranking: generate documents with varying term frequencies, verify ranking order matches BM25 expectations (higher TF-IDF = higher rank)

## Cross-References
- **wa-283h4.11** (LSH error clustering): LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) is used for clustering similar errors in the alien artifact engineering epic. cass's semantic search and LSH error clustering are related search technologies — both find "similar" items in a corpus. Consider sharing the LSH implementation between cass (for session similarity) and the error clustering system (for error similarity). The embedding vectors used by cass could feed directly into LSH buckets.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PurpleLantern","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:12:24.731773Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T20:26:25.165301Z"}

 {"id":"wa-2lp7o","title":"Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure","description":"# Create Cx capability context threading infrastructure

## Background
In asupersync, ALL async effects (spawn, sleep, I/O, trace, checkpoint) are gated through a Cx (capability context) token. Every async function must accept &mut Cx as a parameter. This is fundamentally different from tokio where runtime access is ambient/global.

## What needs to happen
1. Define a project-wide pattern for Cx propagation through the call graph
2. Create a wa-core module (e.g., src/cx.rs or src/context.rs) that wraps and re-exports Cx
3. Establish helper traits/functions that make Cx threading ergonomic
4. Create a RuntimeBuilder wrapper that configures asupersync for FrankenTerm's needs:
   - Worker thread count
   - Poll budget
   - Blocking pool size
   - Presets: current_thread() for tests, multi_thread() for production

## Design considerations
- Cx is &mut (exclusive reference), so it cannot be shared across concurrent tasks without scoping
- Each scope.spawn() gets its own Cx — the runtime manages this
- For existing code that passes Arc<Mutex<T>>, the Cx doesn't replace that — it's orthogonal
- Functions that don't do async effects don't need Cx

## Acceptance criteria
- cx.rs module exists with re-exports and helpers
- RuntimeBuilder configuration works with current_thread() and multi_thread() presets
- A simple integration test can spawn tasks using the new infrastructure
- Documentation comments explain the Cx threading pattern

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Measure Cx propagation overhead through call chains of depth 1, 5, 10, and 20. Target: zero measurable overhead vs direct function calls (Cx is just a reference pass). Add `benches/cx_propagation.rs` using criterion.

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest**: Property tests for capability access correctness — verify that Cx-gated operations are only accessible when a valid Cx is held, and that capability tokens cannot be forged or duplicated. Use `proptest!` macro with arbitrary capability request sequences.

## Concurrency verification
- **Loom model checking**: Use Loom to verify concurrent Cx access safety — specifically that &mut Cx exclusivity is maintained across scope.spawn() boundaries and that no data races are possible when multiple tasks hold their own Cx references simultaneously.","notes":"Implemented Cx infrastructure: added crates/frankenterm-core/src/cx.rs re-exporting Cx/Budget/Scope + runtime types, CxRuntimeBuilder presets/tuning wrapper, and helper APIs (for_testing/with_cx/with_cx_async/spawn_with_cx/try_spawn_with_cx). Added feature-gated integration tests (tests/asupersync_cx_infrastructure.rs), property tests (tests/proptest_cx_capabilities.rs), and benchmark (benches/cx_propagation.rs) with Cargo bench registration. Validation: cargo check --all-targets passed (workspace); targeted feature checks passed for cx bench/tests via cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features asupersync-runtime --bench cx_propagation --test asupersync_cx_infrastructure --test proptest_cx_capabilities and cargo test ... --test proptest_cx_capabilities (4 passed). Full feature all-targets still fails in unrelated in-progress outcome artifacts (wa-d0m4t) and does not block this bead scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:47:51.253433Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:14:42.247715Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:14:42.247649Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2lp7o","depends_on_id":"wa-hj458","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:48:17.308552Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-2mvmx","title":"Watcher clients + per-client view-state model for agent swarms","description":"## Goal\nAdd explicit watcher-client semantics (read-only observers) and per-client view state (active tab/pane/mirror mode) to support multi-agent sessions safely.\n\n## Why\nZellij’s watcher + per-client state model maps directly to FrankenTerm multi-agent orchestration and reduces contention/ambiguity around shared focus.\n\n## Scope\n- Define client roles: interactive vs watcher (read-only).\n- Track per-client view state (active tab/pane, mirrored or independent view).\n- Enforce role-based policy for mutating actions (, pane kill, workflow mutation).\n- Surface role/view information in  and session APIs.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Session/client model update + policy hooks + API exposure.\n- Tests for role enforcement and multi-client focus semantics.\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- Watchers cannot perform mutating actions.\n- Interactive clients preserve current behavior.\n- State visibility is deterministic for orchestration tools.\n\n## Cross-references\n- Zellij session analysis: \n- Session persistence epic: \n- /dp integration epic: ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:42:57.379276Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.185984Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:44:24.166081Z","close_reason":"Superseded by wa-3jewu (same intent, corrected prefix/description)."}

Define capture-path backpressure/failure semantics and verify behavior under overload so recorder does not destabilize core watcher operations.

## Background
Recorder capture must fail safely under stress; silent degradation is unacceptable.

## Scope
- Define queueing and overflow policy (drop/slow/spool) with rationale.
- Define telemetry and alerting conditions.
- Validate with overload and fault injection tests.

## Deliverables
- Backpressure policy doc and implementation plan.
- Overload test scenarios with expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context
The recorder must support multiple storage backends during rollout, including an immediate low-risk path and an advanced FrankenSQLite-backed path. We need a stable contract before implementation to avoid coupling capture, storage internals, and query/index pipelines.

## Scope
Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

## Deliverables
- Trait/interface boundary and data contracts (event envelope + batch semantics)
- Backend selection config model with explicit default/fallback behavior
- Failure taxonomy and retry contract (retryable vs terminal)
- ADR notes on why this interface is shaped for high-throughput append-only ingestion

## Why this matters
A narrow, explicit storage contract prevents premature lock-in and lets us ship incrementally without rewriting capture or query layers.

## Acceptance
- Interface is sufficient for both local append-log backend and FrankenSQLite backend
- Error and durability semantics are explicit and testable
- Feature flags/config behavior are documented and deterministic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhitePuma","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.201142-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:27:26.700401-05:00","close_reason":"Storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration contract documented","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.618035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.658992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. -{"id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","title":"Implement ultra-fast append-only writer hot path for recorder events","description":"## Context
Flight recorder ingestion must remain ultra-low-overhead under swarm-scale burst traffic. The initial backend should be a minimal, append-only write path with predictable latency and minimal allocations.

Implement the hot-path append writer (batched writes, bounded queues, durable flush policy) for canonical recorder events.

- Tunable flush cadence/size thresholds with sane defaults
- Monotonic offset assignment and persisted write checkpoints
- Unit/integration tests for ordering and durability behavior

## Why this matters
This is the performance-critical spine of the recorder. If this path regresses runtime responsiveness, the feature fails regardless of search quality.

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","title":"Implement crash-safe checkpointing and deterministic replay cursor semantics","description":"## Context
Indexer and replay systems require reliable continuation after process restarts/crashes. We need explicit checkpoints and idempotent resume semantics from storage.

## Scope
Implement write/checkpoint metadata and restart replay protocol so downstream consumers can resume from last committed offset without duplication or gaps.

## Deliverables
- Durable checkpoint format and atomic update protocol
- Resume/read APIs for indexers and replayers
- Duplicate suppression strategy where at-least-once replay is unavoidable
- Crash/restart integration tests

## Why this matters
Without deterministic replay/resume semantics, indexing quality and forensic trustworthiness degrade quickly.

## Acceptance
- Restart from crash resumes at deterministic point
- Replay APIs provide no silent gap behavior
- Duplicate handling strategy is explicit and tested","notes":"Claimed to complete replay/checkpoint semantics after wa-oegrb.3.3 closure; fixing partial recorder_storage replay API breakage and adding crash/resume coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented crash-safe checkpoint resume cursor semantics with deterministic replay API and tests","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.393544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Implemented missing replay/checkpoint semantics in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: added ReplayCursor/ReplayBatch/ReplayResumePlan contract types, trait surface (plan_resume/replay_batch), append-log replay reader with deterministic ordinal cursoring and torn-tail-safe behavior, and crash/restart resume tests. Also wired health() to include pipeline metrics default to satisfy expanded health schema. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets (passes); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage -- --nocapture (8 recorder_storage tests passed, including new resume/replay tests).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:52:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","title":"Define retention, partitioning, and archival lifecycle for append-only recorder data","description":"## Context
Full-fidelity recorder data can grow rapidly. Retention and partitioning choices must be first-class so we do not accumulate unbounded costs or operational risk.

## Scope
Define and implement retention windows, partition strategy (time/size), and archival/export hooks for append log segments.

## Deliverables
- Retention policy model (default + override scopes)
- Segment lifecycle logic (active, sealed, archived, purged)
- Tooling hooks for safe archival/export
- Tests proving retention operations preserve invariants and auditability

## Why this matters
Cost and operability determine whether this feature can remain always-on in real swarm usage.

## Acceptance
- Retention behavior is deterministic and observable
- Purge/archive operations do not corrupt offset continuity semantics
- Operators can reason about storage growth via documented policy","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.606628Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.479191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.60657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","title":"Add deep storage-pipeline telemetry, diagnostics, and SLO-oriented metrics","description":"## Context
The writer path is mission-critical; without deep telemetry we cannot tune or safely roll out. We need first-class metrics and tracing for throughput, lag, failures, and durability state.

## Scope
Instrument storage pipeline with metrics/events/logging suitable for SLOs and incident triage.

## Deliverables
- Metrics for append rate, batch size, queue depth, flush latency, error classes, replay lag
- Structured diagnostics for backend health and checkpoint progression
- Alert recommendations for critical degradation patterns
- Documentation mapping metrics to likely remediation actions

## Why this matters
Observability is necessary both for performance tuning and for proving recorder trustworthiness in incidents.

## Acceptance
- Critical failure/lag states are externally visible
- Metrics are stable enough for automated thresholds
- Documentation allows operators to diagnose common failure modes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.570008Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","observability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.523344Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.569934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4","title":"Indexing track: Tantivy lexical indexing, incremental ingestion, rebuild tooling","description":"## Track objective
Design and implement lexical indexing over recorder logs using Tantivy with near-real-time incremental ingestion and robust rebuild/repair workflows.

## Why this track exists
The recorder only creates value if data is rapidly discoverable. Lexical search is the baseline reliability layer and must remain available regardless of semantic subsystem state.

## Deliverables
- Tantivy schema and analyzer strategy
- Incremental indexer pipeline from append-only log
- Query semantics for phrase, boolean, field filters, time windows
- Rebuild/checkpoint/repair tools

## Acceptance criteria
- Lexical search latency and correctness targets are documented and test-enforced.

We need deterministic rebuild/backfill tooling for schema evolution, corruption recovery, and historical imports.

Create tooling to rebuild lexical index from recorder log ranges, with progress reporting and safety checks.

- Full reindex command path with resumable checkpoints
- Partial/range backfill support by time/offset
- Integrity checks comparing index docs to source offsets
- Operator runbook for rebuild scenarios

- Rebuild can be run safely on large datasets

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","title":"Build lexical query service over Tantivy with stable filters/ranking/snippets","description":"## Context
Hybrid search still depends on high-quality lexical retrieval as both baseline and fallback. Query behavior must be explicit, testable, and stable.

## Scope
Implement lexical query service (filters, ranking, snippets/highlights, pagination) over the Tantivy index.

## Deliverables
- Query API for lexical search with deterministic sorting/pagination
- Filter support (time, pane, session, agent, direction, tags)
- Highlight/snippet strategy suitable for terminal text
- Relevance regression tests and fixture corpus

## Why this matters
Lexical search is the reliability anchor; semantic rankers should enhance, not replace, this foundation.

- Lexical results are accurate and predictable

- Fixture-based tests protect behavior from regressions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768296Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","query","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.740105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

- Incident artifact capture for postmortem analysis

- Failure scenarios are reproducible and automated
- Recovery behavior matches documented expectations
- Silent corruption/loss paths are detected and blocked","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:36.905218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:37.301195Z","labels":["chaos","epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.7.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:36.905218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.7.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:37.039907Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.7.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:37.277053Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.7.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:07:37.301113Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.7.5","title":"Wire validation harnesses into CI/nightly with explicit quality gates","description":"## Context
Validation must become an automated quality gate, not a one-time exercise. CI/nightly jobs should guard performance/correctness thresholds.

## Scope
Integrate load, invariant, and quality harnesses into CI/nightly pipelines with explicit pass/fail thresholds.

## Deliverables
- CI/nightly job definitions and environment requirements
- Threshold policy for performance, lag, and correctness
- Artifact retention strategy for failed runs
- Developer guidance for local reproduction of gate failures

## Why this matters
Automation prevents gradual quality erosion and enables confident iteration.

## Acceptance
